title: The One With The Girl From Poughkeepsie

lines.text: Chandler and Joey's, Joey, Chandler, and Phoebe are there as Ross enters.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Chandler, Joey, and Phoebe
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm sorry I'm late, did I miss anything?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Joey stuffing 15 Oreos in his mouth. (Joey, with an obvious mouth full, nods yes.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: 15? (Joey nods again) Your personal best! (Ross takes an Oreo and Joey mumbles, no!)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Where were you?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, on a date. Yeah, I met this girl on the train going to a museum upstate.
simultaneously

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, yeah! How did you meet her?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, which museum?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (just Phoebe) No, answer his.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, it was just me and her at the back of the train, and I sat near the door, so she'd have to pass by me if she wanted to switch cars. She was totally at my mercy.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Were you so late because you were burring this woman?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, I'm getting back down 'cause she lives in Poughkeepsie. She seems really great, but she's like totally great, but she lives two and a half hours away.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: How can she be great if she's from Poughkeepsie? (laughs, at they all look at him) That joke would've killed in Albany.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Done! I did it! Heh, who's stupid now? (He smiles and has cookie remains all over his teeth.)
Opening Credits
Central Perk, Chandler, Joey, Rachel, and Phoebe are there.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, look at this! (Holding a newspaper) They're lighting the big Christmas tree tonight.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Umm, that paper's two weeks old.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: All right, who keeps leaving old newspapers in the trash?! I really wanted to take Kathy to this, I can't believe I missed it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey, y'know, at least you have somebody to miss that stuff with! I hate being alone this time of year! Next thing you know it'll be Valentine's Day, then my birthday, then bang!-before you know it, they're lighting that damn tree again. Ohh, I want somebody! (hearing this, Gunther moves in) Y'know, I want a man!! (Gunther leaves depressed) I mean, it doesn't even have to be a big relationship, y'know, just like a fling would be great.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Really?! I didn't think girls ever just wanted a fling.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, believe me, it's been a long time since I've been flung.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, I know what I'm giving you for Christmas.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Y'know what? There's some nice guys at my office, do you want me to set you up?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah! Wait a minute, it's been a long time that I've been single. How come you never offered this before?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, I have a girlfriend, I'm-I'm happy. So, I no longer feel the need to go out of my way to stop others from being happy.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay! No accountants. Oh, and no one from like legal. I don't like guys with boring jobs.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh and Ross was like what? A lion tamer?
Monica enters

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What's wrong Mon?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ohh, everybody at the restaurant still hates me.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I thought I was making headway, everyone was smiling at me all day, I get off work and I find out that they wrote this (puts on her chef hat) on my chef's hat. (The hat says 'Quit, bitch')

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey, maybe they meant to write, 'Quiet, bitch.'

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey, honey! What's the matter? (Monica shows her, her hat.) Fine, I was just trying to be nice! Whoa!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I mean I have not been picked on this much since kindergarten and they had to bring in someone from junior high to do the see-saw with me. (Joey laughs and Monica glares at him.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ohhh!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I mean they're trying to do everything they can to make me quit, and if there were any other job, I would. But this is something I've been waiting for my whole life.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, wait a minute, you're the boss! Why don't you just yell at them? Or, fire them?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I would love too, but I can't! I mean I just can't, you know that I'm not good at confrontation.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, you know what you can do? I remember reading about this director, I think it was Orson Wells, who at the beginning of the movie would hire somebody, just so he could fire them in front of everybody. Then they would all know, who's boss.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, Mon! I'm not doing anything, why don't you fire me?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That's a good idea! Wait, do you know how to waiter?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Good enough to get fired.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right, you're hired!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey! That must be why I got fired last week! Does this Orson Wells guy direct Burger King commercials?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (he glares at him for a while) Yes.
Chandler's office, he is trying to find Rachel a date.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I say, Drew! Are you seeing anybody right now? (Drew looks at him) Og-ee-op, I'm not asking for me, I'm... I mean... No, I'm-I'm not gay, I'm not asking you out. I'm not-I'm not-I'm not gay!

lines.speaker: Drew
lines.text: I didn't think you were gay. I do now.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: See my friend-my friend, Rachel, she wants to be set up.

lines.speaker: Drew
lines.text: Ahh, I just got out of a big relationship, I'm not looking for any thing serious.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, y'know what, that might be okay even if it was just kind of a fling, that might be all right with Rachel.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Whoa-whoa-whoa-whoa! Is this, hot Rachel, that you took to the Christmas party, Rachel?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (to Drew) Oh, by the way, that is her full name.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Oh wow! I'm free for her!

lines.speaker: Drew
lines.text: Oh, wait a second! I didn't say I wasn't free!

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Hey, Chandler, why don't we talk this over at the Ranger game tomorrow?

lines.speaker: Drew
lines.text: Hold on, y'know I just got a box of Cubans, maybe I bring them by your office around uh, five?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh well, that's uh, a little later than I uh, generally care to stay, but sure!

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Maybe, before the game we could enjoy some eight year old some small batch Basel Hadens.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, I don't really know what that is, but let's!!
Monica and Rachel's, Phoebe is working on a new song.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey! You guys, I'm writing a holiday song for everyone. Do you want to hear it?

lines.speaker: Monica, Rachel, and Joey
lines.text: Yes!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (singing) Happy Chanukah, Monica! May your Christmas be snowy, Joey! Happy New Year, Chandler and Ross. Spin the draddle, Rachel!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Pheebs, that's great!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, yay!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: But y'know umm, Rachel doesn't rhyme with draddle.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I know but it's so hard! Nothing rhymes with your stupid name!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What are you talking about? Lots of things rhyme with Rachel. Bagel. Mail. Jail. Bail. Able. May-pole.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All good, thanks. (to Rachel) Do you maybe have a nickname have like a nickname that's easier to rhyme?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Didn't your dad used to call you Pumpkin?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh yeah!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Pumpkin? Yeah. But did he ever call you like, Budolph?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (entering) Hello, children!

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (to Rachel) Have I got the 50 guys for you!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Really?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh yeah, I just showed this a picture of you and guys were throwing themselves at me! They're buying me drinks! They're giving me stuff! (to Joey) Knicks tonight?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Sure! Where are the seats?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Wherever! I've got like 20!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So, will I like any of these guys?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Y'know what, I'm gonna uh, play the field just a little more.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Chandler!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Guys are signing over their 401-K's to me?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (shocked) You work with robots!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (pause) Yes. (to Rachel) Okay, there's this one guy, Patrick, I think you're gonna like him, he's really nice, he's funny, he's a swimmer.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh, I like swimmer's bodies!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes, and his father invented that magnetic strip on the back of credit cards.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Op, I like credit cards!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: See, I'm not bad at this fixing up thing, huh?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, so what does he do?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, he works in the Fine Foods division.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Your company has a fine foods division?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It's a big company, I don't-if you-I...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Now, wait a second! You make food and robots?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No! No, the robots just work for them.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (getting up) All right, I'm gonna go to work. Does anybody have a problem with that?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, lady, I do! I got a problem with that!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You want a problem? I'll give you a problem!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, what are you gonna do? You're gonna fire me?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You bet your ass, I'm gonna fire you! Thank you.
Central Perk, Ross, Phoebe, and Chandler are there.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, wow! I should get going. I-I got a date tonight.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh yeah! With who?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You know that girl I told you about who lives up in Poughkeepsie?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Not her. Yeah, this is someone else I meet, and I-I can't decide between the two of them. Y'know the one from Poughkeepsie, even though she's a two hour train ride away, is really pretty, really smart, and-and a lot of fun. But this other girl, well, she lives right uptown. Y'know she's, well she's-she's just as pretty, I guess she's smart, she's not fun.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: If she's no fun, why do you want to date her at all?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, I-I want to give her another chance, y'know? She lives so close. And, at the end of the date, the other time, she-she said something that was-if she was kidding was very funny. On the other hand, if she wasn't kidding, she's not fun, she's stupid, and kind of a racist.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (entering) Hey!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, man!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey! Ooh, how was your first day working at the restaurant?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (checks his watch) Damn! (runs out to work)
Allesandro's, Monica is cooking.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (entering from the dining room) Hey.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, what happened to your fancy chef's jacket? (sees there's a burn spot on it)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: They baked it. I can't take this anymore. I'm gonna call a meeting tonight, I'm gonna fire you tonight.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You got it! Oh-oh! (He starts patting the burned spot, which just happens to be over her breast.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What are you doing?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: It's still a tiny bit on fire there.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Thanks. (Joey's still patting the burn spot) I think you got it!
Central Perk, Chandler is there.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (entering) Chandler!! You have the best taste in men!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, like father, like son.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Patrick and I had such a great time last night! I mean I think this could maybe turn into something serious.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Really?! I-I thought you weren't looking for something serious? I thought you were looking for some kind of a fling.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, y'know, possibly. (pause) You didn't tell him that, though? Right?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ummmmmmmm, no.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You told this guy that I was looking for a fling?! You don't tell the guy that!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Why not?! I'd be thrilled if I heard that some hot girl was just looking to get-oh I see.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, between you telling him that I wanted to have a fling and me putting out on the first date-oh, he's so gonna get the wrong idea.
Allesandro's, Joey is eating some cheese.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey, Joey, could you pass the cheese?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah. Listen uh, I'd prefer it if you didn't call me Joey. Since I don't know anyone here, I thought it'd be cool to try out a cool work nickname.

lines.speaker: A Waiter
lines.text: (entering) Hey, dragon! Here's your tips from Monday and Tuesday. (hands him two envelopes)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (opening an envelope) There's like-there's like 300 bucks in this one!

lines.speaker: The Waiter
lines.text: Yeah, people get pretty generous around the holidays. And it never hurts to wear tight trousers.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay. Could the waiters gather around to hear tonight's specials? Okay, first there is a Chilean Sea Bass prepared with a Mango relish on a bag-Why is nobody writing these down?

lines.speaker: The Waiter
lines.text: Because we can remember them.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Because your all gonna make up fake specials and make me cook them like you did the other night?

lines.speaker: The Waiter
lines.text: Well, sure, that too.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, forget the specials for a minute. Umm, all right here's the thing, for the last two weeks I have umm, (quietly) tried really hard to create a positive atmosphere...

lines.speaker: The Waiter
lines.text: Can't hear you!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (louder) A positive atmosphere! But I-I-I have had it up to here. (She holds her hand over her head as an afterthought.) From now on, it is gonna be my way, or the highway! All right? Does anybody have a problem with that?!! (Joey looks at the money he's holding, and doesn't speak up.) Hey new guy! I said, does anybody have a problem with that?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No ma'am.

lines.speaker: The Waiter
lines.text: Hey! He has a name, it's Dragon. Do you wanna know your name? Check your hat. (to another waiter) We did the hat right? (The other waiter nods yes.)
Commercial Break
Allesandro's, continued from earlier. The other waiters are gone and Monica is confronting Joey about his not speaking up.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What the hell happened?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I am so-so-so sorry. I was gonna do it! Really! But I was standing there with 327 dollars in one hand and 238 dollars in the other hand, and I was thinking, "Wow! It's been a long time since I had... (tries to do the math in his head, but can't) 327 + 238 dollars!"

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Joey, we had a deal. That-that's why you're here! I've got to fire you!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: And I gotta pay rent! Look, how-how about this? You don't fire me, instead I stay here, I gain their trust, and they'll start listening to all the nice things I've been saying about you.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What kinda things have you been saying?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well nothing yet, they really hate you and I want to fit in.
Central Perk, Phoebe is working on her holiday song, Chandler is sitting on the couch reading a magazine, and Ross is sleeping on the couch.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (singing) Happy, happy Chanukah, Chandler and Monica. Very merry...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (interrupting) Oh, y'know, y'know what Pheebs?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm not Jewish, so...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So! Ross doesn't really decorate his tree with floss, but you don't hear him complaining do you? God! (Phoebe hits her guitar which wakes up Ross with a start.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Bad dream?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I wasn't sleeping.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh yeah, then uh, what was Phoebe's song about?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: The one with the cat. I gotta go, I've got another date.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So, did you pick one yet?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, it turns out that the one from uptown was making a joke. But it was a different joke than I thought-it wasn't that funny. So I'm still torn.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well look, you don't really like the one from uptown and you're too exhausted from dating the one up in Poughkeepsie, so I say you just end them both. Okay? You take a train up to Poughkeepsie and break up with her, and on your way back you break up with uptown. And then by the time you get home tonight, you're done!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Y'know, you're right. Thank you.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Umm, well I had a similar problem when I lived in Prague.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Prague?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: There's sooo much you don't know.

Chandler and Joey's, Chandler is playing living room golf as Rachel enters. Rachel sees this and holds the door open until Chandler is ready to start his swing, when he is, she slams the door shut which causes the club to fly from his hands. He turns around, shocked.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Chandler! Patrick just uh, ended things with me. Did you or did you not tell him that I was looking for a serious relationship?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I did! I absolutely did!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You idiot!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm sure you're right, but why?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You don't tell a guy that you're looking for a serious relationship! You don't tell the guy that! Now you scared him away!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, man. I'm sorry, I'm so-so sorry.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Y'know, you should never be allowed to talk to people!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I know! I know!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh! See just I'm right back where I started! Aww, this sucks! Being alone, sucks! (She sits down heavily in one of the new chairs)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, y'know, you're-you're gonna meet somebody! You're a great catch! Y'know when I was telling all those guys about you, I didn't have to lie once. (He sits down on the arm of her chair)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah! You graduated Magma Ku Laude, right?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, it doesn't matter. (Kisses her on the top of her head.) Hey, y'know what, I've got two tickets to tonight's Rangers game, you wanna come with me?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Cute guys in little shorts? Sure.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, actually it's a hockey team, so it's angry Canadians with no teeth.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well that sounds fun too. (They hug.)
pause

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Have you ever been with a woman?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?! Chandler, what is the matter with you?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So there is no good time to ask that question.
A train to Poughkeepsie, Ross is asleep against the window.

lines.speaker: The Conductor
lines.text: The next station is Poughkeepsie. Poughkeepsie!

lines.speaker: The Woman From Poughkeepsie
lines.text: (outside Ross's window) Ross? Ross! (she knocks on the window) Wake up! Ross! (the train starts moving) Ross! Ross!! Ross!!! Ross!!!!
Allesandro's, Monica is cooking.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I need more swordfish. (to one of the assistant chefs) Can you get me some more swordfish?

lines.speaker: Kitchen Worker
lines.text: I don't speak English.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You did a minute ago!

lines.speaker: Kitchen Worker
lines.text: Well, I don't know what to tell ya!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Fine!
She goes into the freezer to get it herself, and leaves the door open. The waiter from earlier comes by and closes the door.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay! Very funny! Somebody let me out please?! Come on, I'm cold! (She spills something.) And covered in marinara sauce! Come on! Let me out! (the door opens)

lines.speaker: The Waiter
lines.text: You found that handle, did ya?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That's not funny.

lines.speaker: The Waiter
lines.text: Well that's not true.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (starting to cry) I'm a good person. And I'm a good chef, and I don't deserve to have marinara sauce all over me! Y'know what, if you want me to quit this bad, then all you have to do is...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (interrupting) Hey! Chef Geller! Y'know that little speech you made the other day? Well I got a problem with it!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You do?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You bet I do! I just ah, wasn't listening then, that's all.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well if you want a problem? I'll give you a problem!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What are you gonna do? You're gonna fire me?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You bet your ass I'm gonna fire you! Get out of my kitchen! Get out!! (Joey leaves) All right! Anybody else got a problem? How 'bout you Chuckles? You think this is funny now?

lines.speaker: The Waiter
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: How about if I dance around all covered in sauce? Huh? You think it's funny now?

lines.speaker: The Waiter
lines.text: No, it's really good.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Good! Now, take those salads to table 4, (to the kitchen worker from earlier) And you! Get the swordfish! (to another assistant chef) And you! Get a haircut!
The train, it's pulling into a station.

lines.speaker: The Conductor
lines.text: Last stop, Montreal. This stop is Montreal.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (waking up) What? (notices that there is now a beautiful woman sitting next to him)

lines.speaker: Woman On Train
lines.text: I made a bet with myself that you have beautiful eyes. Now that I see them, I win.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Woman On Train
lines.text: We're at my stop. But would you like to have coffee?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (now fully awake) Are we really in Montreal?!

lines.speaker: Woman On Train
lines.text: Yes we are. So, coffee?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Coffee sounds great. (They get up) Wait, so, so you live in Montreal?

lines.speaker: Woman On Train
lines.text: Oh, no. But it's just a two hour ferry ride to Nova Scotia.
Allesandro's, Joey is coming back in with his coat on.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well I guess I should've thought about my wife and kids before I talked back to chef Geller!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yep! Looks like it's gonna be a leeeeean Christmas at the Dragon house this year.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Enough!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (leaving) Lean-lean-lean!
Closing Credits
Central Perk, Phoebe is singing her holiday song.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (singing) "Went to the store, sat on Santa's lap.
Asked him to bring my friends all kind of crap.
Said all you need is to write them a song.
They haven't heard it, so don't try and sing along.
No, don't sing along.
Monica, Moncia, have a happy Chanukah.
Saw Santa Clause, he said hello to Ross.
And plese tell Joey, Christmas will be snowy!
And Rachel and Chandler, have err-umm-glander!!"
Happy holidays, everybody!
End
